mnemonics.title: WINNER Tree - MIN value wins
mnemonics.content: W-I-N-N-E-R: Winners In Nodes store the element with the Lowest value. Think: 'Winners Need Everyone's Results' - the smaller value advances upward.

mnemonics.title: LOSER Tree - MAX value stored
mnemonics.content: L-O-S-E-R: Losers Often Stay Existing in the Result. Each node stores the larger element (the loser) while winners propagate. Remember: 'Losers Keep Swapping Entries Recursively' as they get beaten.

mnemonics.title: Update Complexity
mnemonics.content: Height equals log of leaves, Update takes that many comparisons: HALT - Height = Log And Log Takes time. Each level = 1 comparison.

acronyms.usage: Used for k-way merging of sorted sequences, storing winner/loser at internal nodes
Min Selection Tree where parent stores smaller child; root has minimum of all
Max Selection Tree variant where parent stores larger child; more efficient updates
Merging k sorted lists into one sorted list using Selection Trees

visualTips.visualization: Think of the tree as a tournament bracket. Leaves are players, internal nodes are match results. Winner tree: champion at top. Draw it as a triangle with wider base - leaves at bottom, root at apex.
When updating, trace ONE path from changed leaf to root (like a tree trunk to crown). Only nodes on this path need re-evaluation. Draw the path in a different color to visualize.
Draw extra 'ghost' leaves with +∞ value. They always lose but fill the tree to become a complete binary tree. Use dashed lines for dummy leaves.

analogies.analogy: Selection Trees are like a single-elimination tournament. The winner (smallest number) wins each match and advances. When a winner is eliminated (output), only their 'branch' of the tournament needs replay, not the entire tournament.

Think of k sorted lists as assembly lines producing components. Selection Tree is like a 'foreman' who always knows which line has the next smallest component, calling for it. When that line produces the next component, the foreman updates their knowledge.

analogies.analoy: Each sorted list is a runner carrying a baton (element). The Selection Tree is like the race official tracking who has the smallest time. When the fastest runner finishes (element output), they get the next baton from their team, and the official re-evaluates only that lane.
